spec_match.rationale: Covers the spec narratively but the concept page is largely prose — relevant headline capabilities of the Closes the Incognito-detection vector in StorageManager.estimate by applying the disk-bucketed quota in both modes. are explained but not exercised on this page.

interactive.rationale: Has some interactive surface but is closer to a styled reference page than a hands-on demo — light controls, mostly narrative.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index closely matches what the concept page delivers — title, lede, and interactive surface line up.

edge_cases.rationale: Page focuses on the canonical happy-path framing; spec edge cases are noted but not exercised live.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature link in the see-also section as the design system requires.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex creeping in, button colours and contrast appear within AA.

openQuestions.title: Add a live estimate() readout next to the static numbers
openQuestions.detail: Everything else in the v151 set has a live API call. Even a single navigator.storage.estimate() readout inline would lift this from a static post into a working demonstration without changing the page's narrative shape.

openQuestions.title: Add at least one live probe so the page is hands-on, not just narrative
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md rule 7 says every concept must be interactive. Even an explainer / reference / guide page should ship one live probe (feature detect, API call, observer readout) so the visitor leaves having interacted with the actual API surface.

summary: Step-by-step narrative with concrete numbers (120 MB on a 16 GB device, 10 GiB floor). Main gaps: Add a live estimate() readout next to the static numbers; Add at least one live probe so the page is hands-on, not just narrative.
